question: When Jesus calms the storm and the disciples ask ‘Who then is this?’, what OT portrait of God (who rules the sea) is being quietly invoked—and why is that not a small claim?

choices.A: OT texts depict YHWH as the one who stills seas; portraying Jesus doing this suggests divine authority over creation.
choices.B: It only shows Jesus is a skilled sailor.
choices.C: It is metaphorical and no miracle is claimed.
choices.D: It shows the disciples are exaggerating.

teaching.title: Sea-stilling as divine prerogative
teaching.body: In Israel’s scriptures, mastery over chaotic waters is a signature of God’s power. The Synoptic narratives frame Jesus exercising that same authority, provoking the disciples’ identity-question. The point isn’t ‘wow, weather control’—it’s ‘what category of being commands creation like this?’
